channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources worldwide

item.title: S&P Maintains Israel Credit Rating at A Despite Iran War
item.description: S&P Global Ratings affirmed Israel's A/A-1 credit rating with stable outlook, citing resilience despite ongoing Iran conflict and regional tensions.

item.title: Japan's Mogami-Class Warship Favored for New Zealand Frigate Competition
item.description: Japan's Mogami-class frigate has emerged as the leading contender for New Zealand's next-generation warship program, competing against Britain's Type 31 design, with Australia potentially influencing the outcome.

item.title: US to Host Israel-Lebanon Talks Pressing for Hezbollah Disarmament
item.description: Washington will host new Israel-Lebanon negotiations as the Trump team pushes for Hezbollah disarmament, while awaiting Iran's response to a US proposal to end regional conflict.

item.title: Victory Day Ceasefire Confusion as Both Ukraine and Russia Report Continued Fighting
item.description: Despite agreement on a 3-day ceasefire and prisoner swap, over 140 strikes were recorded on Ukrainian positions by 7am, with both sides reporting drone attacks and front-line fighting.

item.title: Israel Wounds 9 Palestinians Including Child in Gaza Ceasefire Violation
item.description: Gaza Civil Defense reports that Israeli forces wounded 9 Palestinians, including a child, in the latest violation of the ceasefire agreement.

item.title: Pope Leo After Rubio Meeting Asks God to Inspire Leaders to Calm Tensions
item.description: Pope Leo met with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and appealed for divine inspiration to help calm escalating Middle East tensions.

item.title: Meloni Meets Rubio as Iran War Strains Italy-US Ties
item.description: Italian PM Meloni met with Secretary Rubio amid questions about allied support for US efforts to confront Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Russia Ukraine Accuse Each Other of Violating Ceasefire
item.description: Both Russia and Ukraine accused each other of violating the Victory Day ceasefire agreement, continuing attacks despite the truce.

item.title: Ukraine Says It Is Running Short on Air Defence Missiles
item.description: Ukraine reports critical shortages of air defense missiles as Russian attacks continue, raising concerns about its ability to protect critical infrastructure.

item.title: US Concerned by Taiwan Defence Delay Seen as Concession to China
item.description: The US expressed concerns that delays in Taiwan's defense capabilities could be seen as concessions to China amid rising regional tensions.

item.title: Paraguay Deeply Values Taiwan Ties President Says in Taipei
item.description: Paraguay's president reaffirmed strong ties with Taiwan during his Taipei visit, emphasizing the importance of the diplomatic relationship.

item.title: British MPs Visit China for First Time in Seven Years
item.description: British parliamentarians visited China for the first time in seven years amid efforts to maintain diplomatic dialogue between the countries.

item.title: ASEAN Leaders to Create Shared Fuel Reserve for Future Crises
item.description: Southeast Asian leaders agreed to establish a regional fuel-sharing framework to address energy security concerns amid Middle East supply disruptions.

item.title: US Imposes Sanctions on 10 Individuals and Companies Aiding Iran's Weapons Sector
item.description: The US Treasury sanctioned 10 individuals and entities for providing material support to Iran's weapons development programs.

item.title: UAE Countering Iranian Air Attack After Trump Says Ceasefire Still in Effect
item.description: The UAE activated air defense systems against renewed Iranian attacks even as Trump stated the ceasefire remained in effect.

item.title: US Revises UN Resolution on Iran as China and Russia Expected to Veto
item.description: The US revised its UN Security Council resolution on Iran, though China and Russia are still expected to veto the measure.

item.title: Taiwan Parliament Approves Extra Defence Spending Less Than Government Wanted
item.description: Taiwan's legislature approved additional defense spending, though less than the government's original request amid budget constraints.

item.title: Divided Kingdom Pro Independence Parties Surge Across Britain
item.description: Pro-independence parties made significant gains across Britain in recent elections, reflecting growing regional divisions within the UK.

item.title: Anders Fogh Rasmussen Interview NATO Prepares for New Security Reality
item.description: Former NATO Secretary General discusses how the alliance is adapting to the new security landscape amid Iran tensions and Ukraine war.

item.title: US Forces Strike Two Empty Iranian Oil Tankers Central Command Says
item.description: US Central Command confirmed strikes on two Iranian oil tankers in the Gulf of Oman as part of efforts to enforce the Hormuz blockade.

item.title: France Arrests Ukrainian Linked to Notorious Russian Torture Prison in Donetsk
item.description: French authorities arrested a Ukrainian national accused of involvement in the notorious Izolyatsia torture prison in Russian-occupied Donetsk.

item.title: Massive Forest Fire Burns Near Ukraine Border With Russia as FPV Drones Block Access
item.description: A large forest fire near Ukraine's border with Russia is being exacerbated by FPV drones blocking firefighter access to the area.

item.title: EU Ready to Help Belgium as Russian Oligarchs Reopen Frozen Assets Fight
item.description: The EU offered assistance to Belgium as Russian oligarchs renewed legal challenges to unfreeze their sanctioned assets.

item.title: India April Consumer Inflation Likely Rose to 3.8 Percent as Higher Fuel Costs Weigh
item.description: India's consumer inflation is expected to have risen to 3.8% in April as higher fuel costs from Middle East tensions impact prices.

item.title: Markets Understate Scope of UK Dysfunction
item.description: Analysis suggests financial markets may be understating the extent of UK political and economic dysfunction amid governance challenges.
